reasons: Tigreal can group and control enemies, giving Bane a clear AoE damage window after the engage.
Bane can add follow-up damage when Tigreal forces enemies to stay inside a teamfight area.

proof.summary: Tigreal starts the fight and forces enemies into a grouped position, while Bane follows with AoE damage during the control window.
worksBestWhen: Tigreal engages after Bane is close enough to follow up.
Bane saves his AoE damage for the confirmed crowd-control window.
failureCases: Tigreal engages too far from Bane.
The enemy spreads out before Tigreal commits.

reasons: Atlas can pull enemies into one area, helping Bane punish grouped targets with AoE follow-up.
Atlas gives Bane a stronger teamfight entry point when enemies are already displaced and controlled.

proof.summary: Atlas creates the grouped setup, while Bane adds damage after enemies are displaced and controlled.
worksBestWhen: Bane waits until Atlas finishes the pull before committing damage.
failureCases: Bane is zoned away before the follow-up.

reasons: Minotaur gives Bane a durable frontline and sustain support for longer objective fights.
Minotaur's control windows let Bane follow with damage without being the first hero to absorb pressure.

proof.summary: Minotaur absorbs pressure, heals nearby teammates, and creates crowd-control windows that let Bane follow with damage.
worksBestWhen: Minotaur stays between Bane and enemy divers.
Bane plays around Minotaur's engage and heal timing.
failureCases: Bane walks too far ahead of Minotaur.
Minotaur's key control is unavailable when Bane commits.

reasons: Rafaela's heal and movement support help Bane chase, kite, and stay active during extended skirmishes.
Bane benefits from Rafaela's sustain when he has to keep fighting around objectives or lane pressure.

proof.summary: Rafaela improves Bane's extended-fight uptime by helping him reposition and recover during poke or chase sequences.
worksBestWhen: Rafaela stays close enough to support Bane's re-entry.
Bane uses the speed window to either chase or disengage.
failureCases: Bane dives beyond Rafaela's support range.
The enemy bursts Bane before the sustain matters.

reasons: Angela can attach to Bane during a commit, giving him extra protection while he fights inside enemy range.
Angela's support helps Bane stay alive long enough to finish his damage cycle after entering the fight.

proof.summary: Angela supports Bane's forward pressure by adding shield and sustain during the moment Bane commits into a fight.
worksBestWhen: Angela saves her ultimate for Bane's real engage.
Bane has enough damage and cooldowns ready after Angela joins.
failureCases: Angela attaches after Bane is already controlled.
Bane commits without a clear target or follow-up.
